fields.summary: Protocol changes for RM to bind and notify a restarted AM of existing containers

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description:
For long lived containers we don't want the AM to be a SPOF.

When the RM restarts a (failed) AM, it should be given the list of containers it had already been allocated. the AM should then be able to contact the NMs to get details on them. NMs would also need to do any binding of the containers needed to handle a moved/restarted AM.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Roll up for long-lived services in YARN

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

comments.body:
This is not something that MR could (currently) handle unless it was persisting its state somewhere, but for use cases like running HBase on YARN, we'd want to get a list of the containers running the AMs, so that the AM could bond to them again.

To avoid having to persist any run-time state in an AM, the AM should be able to set/query some limited metadata to every container -such as adding a container role string/int that could differentiate the purpose of an existing container.

Currently, if an AM wants to support failover/recovery, it is its responsibility of the AM to persist all resource-requests and allocations? To me this seems a reasonable separation of responsibilities, why do you want to change it?
AFAIK, currently, if the AM fails then all its allocations are released.

I'd add that discarding outstanding requests on AM restart would avoid the AM from having the problem of handling container allocations which it was not expecting. After enumerating the active set of containers, the AM could make its own decisions about how many containers it needs -and where.
I'd like to take over this, thanks.

Uploaded  a patch for changing AM protocols to get the previous running containers on registration.
The uploaded patch is based on YARN-1490 and may not apply locally for now. Just to give an early view of the patch.
thanks for the comments, will update the patch once YARN-1490 gets in

Looks good as an API for an AM to work with

# there's a lot of duplication across each scheduler's {{ getAllRunningContainers()}} -could it be stuck somewhere shared?
# I get the impression that the protobuf response when there are no running containers in {{null}}. An empty list would be more elegant AM-side as it wouldn't need to ever check for null/have a risk of NPEs

bq. there's a lot of duplication across each scheduler's {{ getAllRunningContainers()}} -could it be stuck somewhere shared?
right, there is a lot of duplications not only this methods, but some other methods also. I wanted to change YarnScheduler to be an abstract class and put some common methods inside it, thoughts?

We can't change {{YarnScheduler}} from an interface to a class, as we don't know what has subclassed it outside the hadoop core codebase. 

What you could do is have a class {{AbstractYarnScheduler extends YarnScheduler}}, base the standard schedulers off there and put the code in that base class

Knowing well this is an early patch, quick review comments
 - Document RegisterApplicationMasterResponse.getRunningContainers() with its semantics. Particularly about allocated, acquired, reserved containers from previous AM being killed.
 - yarn_service_protos.proto: running_containers -> containers_from_previous_attempt ? Running or not may(or may not) change depending on the implementation. If we do this, we should change the name everywhere.
 - YarnScheduler.getAllRunningContainers(): Rename and also deal with an app-attempt instead of application? Because that is how scheduler looks at containers - per attempt.
 - Per [YARN-1490 comment|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-1490?focusedCommentId=13866267&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-13866267], we need to take care of NMTokens too.

The code duplication in schedulers is a bigger problem that needs to be addressed. But it has to start somewhere. I am +1 for starting with a AbstractYarnScheduler.
New patch fixed the comments, created a new AbstractYarnScheduler for putting the common methods

More comments on the patch:
 - FifoScheduler depends on a concurrentSkipList. So you can't put the initiation in the base class. You can leave the declaration though.
 - There are some long lines in the patch.
 - We don't need two APIs in RegResponse.newInstance(). They are not supposed to be used by users.
 - We don't need the explicit state-check in the method in the base scheduler class.
Fixed the recent comments, filed YARN-1588 for taking care of the NMTokens

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body:
This is not something that MR could (currently) handle unless it was persisting its state somewhere, but for use cases like running HBase on YARN, we'd want to get a list of the containers running the AMs, so that the AM could bond to them again.

To avoid having to persist any run-time state in an AM, the AM should be able to set/query some limited metadata to every container -such as adding a container role string/int that could differentiate the purpose of an existing container.

Currently, if an AM wants to support failover/recovery, it is its responsibility of the AM to persist all resource-requests and allocations? To me this seems a reasonable separation of responsibilities, why do you want to change it?
AFAIK, currently, if the AM fails then all its allocations are released.

I'd add that discarding outstanding requests on AM restart would avoid the AM from having the problem of handling container allocations which it was not expecting. After enumerating the active set of containers, the AM could make its own decisions about how many containers it needs -and where.
I'd like to take over this, thanks.

Uploaded  a patch for changing AM protocols to get the previous running containers on registration.
The uploaded patch is based on YARN-1490 and may not apply locally for now. Just to give an early view of the patch.
thanks for the comments, will update the patch once YARN-1490 gets in

Looks good as an API for an AM to work with

# there's a lot of duplication across each scheduler's {{ getAllRunningContainers()}} -could it be stuck somewhere shared?
# I get the impression that the protobuf response when there are no running containers in {{null}}. An empty list would be more elegant AM-side as it wouldn't need to ever check for null/have a risk of NPEs

bq. there's a lot of duplication across each scheduler's {{ getAllRunningContainers()}} -could it be stuck somewhere shared?
right, there is a lot of duplications not only this methods, but some other methods also. I wanted to change YarnScheduler to be an abstract class and put some common methods inside it, thoughts?

We can't change {{YarnScheduler}} from an interface to a class, as we don't know what has subclassed it outside the hadoop core codebase. 

What you could do is have a class {{AbstractYarnScheduler extends YarnScheduler}}, base the standard schedulers off there and put the code in that base class

Knowing well this is an early patch, quick review comments
 - Document RegisterApplicationMasterResponse.getRunningContainers() with its semantics. Particularly about allocated, acquired, reserved containers from previous AM being killed.
 - yarn_service_protos.proto: running_containers -> containers_from_previous_attempt ? Running or not may(or may not) change depending on the implementation. If we do this, we should change the name everywhere.
 - YarnScheduler.getAllRunningContainers(): Rename and also deal with an app-attempt instead of application? Because that is how scheduler looks at containers - per attempt.
 - Per [YARN-1490 comment|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-1490?focusedCommentId=13866267&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-13866267], we need to take care of NMTokens too.

The code duplication in schedulers is a bigger problem that needs to be addressed. But it has to start somewhere. I am +1 for starting with a AbstractYarnScheduler.
New patch fixed the comments, created a new AbstractYarnScheduler for putting the common methods

More comments on the patch:
 - FifoScheduler depends on a concurrentSkipList. So you can't put the initiation in the base class. You can leave the declaration though.
 - There are some long lines in the patch.
 - We don't need two APIs in RegResponse.newInstance(). They are not supposed to be used by users.
 - We don't need the explicit state-check in the method in the base scheduler class.
Fixed the recent comments, filed YARN-1588 for taking care of the NMTokens
